perturbation.original_text: 3. If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any Co nfidential Material, the Stockholder will provide the Company with prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any Confidential Material, the Stockholder will use its best efforts to provide the Company with notice (i.e., within 5 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, demand, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement; provided, however, that failure to provide such notice will not be a breach of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires 'prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days)' but the changed text extends this to 'within 5 business days' and adds 'failure to provide such notice will not be a breach of this Agreement.' This contradicts the original intent of prompt notification and creates uncertainty about the repercussions of delayed notification, thus the purpose of the clause is contradited, weakening enforcement of the confidentiality terms. The contradiction is within section 3 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any Confidential Material, the Stockholder will use its best efforts to provide the Company with notice (i.e., within 5 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, demand, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement; provided, however, that failure to provide such notice will not be a breach of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notification period from 'prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days)' to 'within 5 business days' and adds a clause stating that failure to provide notice is not a breach. This directly contradicts the original intent of ensuring prompt notification, creating uncertainty about the consequences of delayed notice. The contradiction lies in the fact that the original clause implies a breach for failing to provide prompt notice, while the changed clause explicitly states otherwise, thus contradicting its original purpose within the same section (section 3).

perturbation.original_text: 5. Each Party agrees that the Company does no t have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Company will suffer irreparable harm and will be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to any other remedies to which the Company ma y be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Each Party agrees that the Company may not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Company will suffer irreparable harm and may be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement unless otherwise specified in writing and agreed upon by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'does not have' to 'may not have' and 'will be entitled' to 'may be entitled' introduces uncertainty about the company's legal recourse. Furthermore, stating that remedies 'shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies' contradicts the original statement that remedies 'shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies'. This creates an in-text contradiction within section 5 regarding the Company's ability to seek legal remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Each Party agrees that the Company may not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Company will suffer irreparable harm and may be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement unless otherwise specified in writing and agreed upon by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text modifies section 5 of the contract. The original section 5 stated the Company 'does not have an adequate remedy at law' and 'will be entitled to specific performance'. The Changed Text uses 'may not have' and 'may be entitled', which introduces ambiguity and uncertainty. Further, the original section 5 states that remedies 'shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies'. The Changed Text then states 'Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies unless otherwise specified in writing'. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the exact same section, these changes create a clear and direct contradiction within section 5 itself, leading to uncertainty about the Company's legal recourse. This fits the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, as the key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently within the same section, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement may not be amended, chan ged, waived, discharged, or terminated (except as explicitly provided herein), in wh ole or in part, except by an instrument in writing that refers specifically to this Agre ement and that is signed by the Party against whom enforcement of such amendment, chan ge, waiver, discharge, or termination is sought. The waiver by any Party of any breach of this Agreement will not be deemed or construed as a waiver of any other breach of this Agreement, whether prior, subsequent, or contemporaneous. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement may be amended, changed, waived, discharged, or terminated (except as explicitly provided herein), in whole or in part, either by an instrument in writing that refers specifically to this Agreement and that is signed by both Parties or by mutual oral agreement. The waiver by any Party of any breach of this Agreement will be deemed a waiver of similar future breaches. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof unless explicitly stated otherwise in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original text by allowing amendments through 'mutual oral agreement' instead of requiring a written instrument. Also, 'deemed a waiver of similar future breaches' is contradicting the original text, where it says that it 'will not be deemed or construed as a waiver of any other breach of this Agreement'. This creates an in-text contradiction that leads to ambiguities about amending the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Agreement may be amended, changed, waived, discharged, or terminated (except as explicitly provided herein), in whole or in part, either by an instrument in writing that refers specifically to this Agreement and that is signed by both Parties or by mutual oral agreement. The waiver by any Party of any breach of this Agreement will be deemed a waiver of similar future breaches. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof unless explicitly stated otherwise in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6 introduces two strong contradictions. First, it allows amendments through 'mutual oral agreement,' which directly conflicts with the initial requirement of a 'written instrument'. Second, it states that a waiver of a breach 'will be deemed a waiver of similar future breaches,' contradicting the original text's explicit statement that such a waiver 'will not be deemed or construed as a waiver of any other breach'. The contradicted text is the Changed Text itself, since the Changed Text now allows amendments through 'mutual oral agreement' while the original says that it is not allowed, and the waiver of a breach 'will be deemed a waiver of similar future breaches,' where the original text 'will not be deemed or construed as a waiver of any other breach of this Agreement'

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement shall be governed by and cons trued in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, with regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: Switching the governing law from Delaware to New York directly conflicts with the original text. Since, based on the contract, it was originally governed by Delaware laws, but now it is changed to New York. This in-text contradiction impacts the interpretation and enforcement of the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, with regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from Delaware to New York. This directly contradicts the original agreement, impacting the interpretation and enforcement, as the agreement cannot simultaneously be governed by both Delaware and New York law. While the changed text and contradicted text are identical, this does not mean a contradiction was not introduced. Based on the explanation provided, the switch from Delaware to New York directly conflicts with the original agreement, causing the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all pr ior agreements and understandings, written and oral, between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof, except for any prior oral agreements made between the parties relating to confidentiality of stockholder information. This supersedes all prior written agreements and understandings between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'except for any prior oral agreements made between the parties relating to confidentiality of stockholder information' directly contradicts the first part of the sentence. This creates a contradiction as to what constitutes the entire agreement, which introduces ambiguity that makes it hard to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof, except for any prior oral agreements made between the parties relating to confidentiality of stockholder information. This supersedes all prior written agreements and understandings between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original 'entire agreement' clause to include an exception for prior oral agreements related to confidentiality. The original text states that the agreement supersedes all prior agreements, both written and oral, but the changed text creates an exception, explicitly contradicting the original clause. This creates a direct conflict within the document, making it unclear what constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. The changed text and contradicted text are identical, indicating that the change itself is a contradiction of itself. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
